metadata.title:
TRACTION CD.


IS STUBBORN

DECLINES TO MAKE WRITTEN
AGREEMENT ON BRIDGE
COST-ONE BILL TO BE
COLLECTED FROM w. W..
p. c.

pages.raw_text:
Why has not the Traction Co.
put into writing it's offer to pay its
proportionate share of the cost of
the new concrete bridge on Wash
ington St?


Several months ago the council
appointed committee composed
of Mayor Moore, Commissioner
ONO, Pres. Pratt and Councilmen
Phillips, Baldwin and Gray as a
committee to put the streetcar
companies on record.


The Washington Water Power
Co. put into writing an agreement
to pay it's proportionate Share of
the cost of the proposed new How
ard st. bridges. But the best the
committee has gotten from the
Traction people is that it wants
the city to give it credit for what
ever it pays on the Washington st.
bridge on the proposed viaduct
which is to extend from the bridge
to the hill and passing over the
Spokane International, Great
Northern and O. r. & N. lines.


The committee asked the Trac-
ton people for an agreement to pay
its share Of the bridge cost.


Notwithstanding the fact that
the w. w. p. has given its written
agreement for a share of the cost
on the Howard st. bridges, its sim
cerity is doubtful, owing to its ac-
tons with regard to the cost of
the temporary bridge on Howard
st. which replaces the structure
that one of it's cars knocked down.
1t was determined that the COM
pany's share of the temporary
bridge was $1,700. Payment was
refused.


The board of works has put the
matter into the hands of the cor
poration counsel for collection
through the courts.
